raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/XXXX, a charge of {$890.00} from XXXX was posted to my Citi credit card through a recurring payment arrangement. I disputed this charge with Citibank because the renewed charge was not for the same plan and terms as my original purchase. 

My original Wix purchase in XX/XX/XXXX was for a XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX at a total price of {$340.00}. In XXXX, XXXX migrated that plan to a XXXX XXXX XXXX and charged {$890.00} for another 3-year term. This was a materially different plan at a substantially higher price. 

I disputed the charge with Citibank and provided supporting documents, and I explained that this was not simply a routine renewal of the exact same plan under the exact same terms. The merchant changed the plan and increased the price dramatically, and I did not knowingly agree to those material changes. 

Citibank denied my dispute. I do not believe Citibank fairly evaluated the evidence or adequately considered that the merchant had migrated my subscription to a different plan at a much higher price. I also contacted the merchant directly to request a refund, but the merchant refused. 

I am submitting this complaint because I believe Citibank failed to reasonably investigate the dispute and improperly treated this as a normal recurring charge, even though the merchant had changed the underlying plan and significantly increased the price.
